sources.label: TKH Group newsroom / AGM 2026 (dividend paid Jun 2, pre-window)
TKH Q1 2026 market update - Electrification separation on plan (May)
TKH strategy: separate Electrification, Automation focus (background)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; the Electrification dual-track separation (transaction targeted Q4 2026, talks with interested parties since mid-Q2) is progressing exactly as already priced into the latent-value thesis, and today's -1.5% drift does not change the setup versus yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: The Q4 2026 Electrification transaction crystallizes hidden value and leaves a focused Automation/machine-vision pure-play with AI-integrated inspection technology, at a still-modest $2.2B cap that screens as genuinely under-owned.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but catalyst is months out; Q1 2026 showed turnover and result growth with improved Electrification performance, and the stock drifts (+15.7% YTD) until separation news lands.
verdicts.bear: A weak price or failed dual-track outcome for Electrification, or a slower machine-vision recovery, would deflate the sum-of-the-parts case and leave a mid-cap industrial with cyclical exposure.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Electrification separation transaction announcement targeted for Q4 2026 (dual-track sale/listing).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Dual-track process slips or fetches a low valuation, undermining the value-crystallization thesis.
